paragraphs.en: My name is Tom. I am twelve years old. I go to school every day from Monday to Friday. My school is not far from my house. I walk to school with my friend Lucy.
paragraphs.fr: Je m'appelle Tom. J'ai douze ans. Je vais a l'ecole tous les jours du lundi au vendredi. Mon ecole n'est pas loin de ma maison. Je marche jusqu'a l'ecole avec mon amie Lucy.

paragraphs.en: School starts at half past eight. My first lesson is maths. I like maths because the teacher is very nice. She teaches us numbers and shapes. After maths, we have English. English is my favourite subject.
paragraphs.fr: L'ecole commence a huit heures et demie. Mon premier cours est les maths. J'aime les maths parce que la professeure est tres gentille. Elle nous apprend les nombres et les formes. Apres les maths, nous avons anglais. L'anglais est ma matiere preferee.

paragraphs.en: At half past ten, it is time for a break. I play in the playground with my friends. We eat a snack and we talk about our favourite games. The break is only fifteen minutes, but it is fun.
paragraphs.fr: A dix heures et demie, c'est l'heure de la pause. Je joue dans la cour de recreation avec mes amis. Nous mangeons un gouter et nous parlons de nos jeux preferes. La pause ne dure que quinze minutes, mais c'est amusant.

paragraphs.en: In the afternoon, we have science and art. I like art because I can draw and paint. My teacher, Mr Brown, always says I am good at drawing. He likes my pictures very much.
paragraphs.fr: L'apres-midi, nous avons sciences et arts plastiques. J'aime les arts plastiques parce que je peux dessiner et peindre. Mon professeur, M. Brown, dit toujours que je suis bon en dessin. Il aime beaucoup mes dessins.

paragraphs.en: After school, I go home and do my homework. Sometimes it is easy, sometimes it is difficult. After my homework, I play with my dog in the garden. I love my school day!
paragraphs.fr: Apres l'ecole, je rentre a la maison et je fais mes devoirs. Parfois c'est facile, parfois c'est difficile. Apres mes devoirs, je joue avec mon chien dans le jardin. J'adore ma journee d'ecole !

vocabulary.definition_en: une salle dans une ecole ou les eleves ont cours
vocabulary.example: There are twenty desks in our classroom.

vocabulary.definition_en: une personne qui enseigne aux eleves a l'ecole
vocabulary.example: My teacher is very kind and helpful.

vocabulary.definition_en: l'etude des nombres, des formes et des quantites
vocabulary.example: We have maths every Monday morning.

vocabulary.definition_en: la langue parlee en Angleterre, aux Etats-Unis, etc.
vocabulary.example: I study English at school three times a week.

vocabulary.definition_en: une courte periode de repos entre les cours
vocabulary.example: We have a break at ten o'clock.

vocabulary.definition_en: du travail qu'un professeur donne aux eleves a faire a la maison
vocabulary.example: I do my homework every evening after dinner.

vocabulary.definition_en: celui ou celle que l'on aime le plus
une periode de temps pendant laquelle un professeur enseigne aux eleves
vocabulary.example: Our English lesson is at nine o'clock.

grammar_points.title: Present Simple - 3rd Person Singular
grammar_points.explanation: En anglais, quand on parle de **he**, **she** ou **it** au present simple, on ajoute **-s** ou **-es** au verbe. Par exemple : 'I teach' mais 'She teach**es**'. 'I like' mais 'He like**s**'. C'est different du francais ou les terminaisons des verbes changent pour chaque personne.
examples: The school starts at half past eight. (start → starts)

grammar_points.title: Telling the Time with 'half past' and 'o'clock'
grammar_points.explanation: En anglais, on dit **'o'clock'** pour les heures exactes et **'half past'** pour trente minutes apres l'heure. Par exemple : 'eight o'clock' = 8:00, 'half past eight' = 8:30.
examples: School starts at half past eight. (= 8:30)
We have a break at half past ten. (= 10:30)
I wake up at seven o'clock. (= 7:00)

expressions.example: My favourite subject is English because I love reading.
expressions.note: Une facon tres courante de parler de ce que l'on prefere a l'ecole.

expressions.example: After school, I play football with my friends.
expressions.note: S'utilise pour decrire ce que l'on fait quand la journee d'ecole est terminee.

expressions.example: It's time for lunch! Let's go to the canteen.
expressions.note: S'utilise pour dire que quelque chose devrait se passer maintenant.

expressions.example: I am good at drawing, but I am not good at singing.
expressions.note: Utilisez 'good at' + nom ou verbe en -ing pour parler de vos capacites.

written.question: What is Tom's favourite subject?
written.explanation: Tom says 'English is my favourite subject' in the second paragraph.

written.question: Complete: 'She _____ us maths every day.'
written.explanation: With 'she' (3rd person singular), we add -es to verbs ending in -ch: teach → teaches.

written.question: What does Tom do after school?
written.explanation: Tom says 'After school, I go home and do my homework.'

written.sentence: My teacher ________ (like) my drawings very much.
written.hint: Remember to add -s for he/she/it in the present simple.

written.sentence: School ________ (start) at half past eight every morning.
written.hint: 'School' is like 'it' — use the 3rd person singular form.

written.french: Ma matiere preferee est l'anglais. Mon professeur est tres gentille.
written.suggested_answer: My favourite subject is English. My teacher is very nice.

oral.instruction: Read this passage aloud slowly and clearly.
oral.text: School starts at half past eight. My first lesson is maths. I like maths because the teacher is very nice. She teaches us numbers and shapes.
oral.focus: Pay attention to the 's' sound at the end of 'starts', 'teaches' and 'shapes'. Pronounce each word clearly.

oral.question: What is your favourite subject at school? What time does your school start?
oral.suggested_answer: My favourite subject is English. I like it because the teacher is very nice. My school starts at eight o'clock in the morning.

oral.question: Describe your school day. What do you do in the morning and in the afternoon?
oral.suggested_answer: In the morning, I have maths and English. At half past ten, we have a break. In the afternoon, I have science and art. After school, I do my homework.
